activities_projects.title: Stochasticity in DNA Replication
activities_projects.objective: Analyze why variation is an inherent byproduct of biochemical DNA copying
materials: Research papers on DNA Polymerase error rates
steps: Research the biochemical steps of DNA replication and the role of enzymes.
Explain the concept of 'thermal fluctuations' leading to base-pair mismatches.
Calculate the statistical probability of a mutation if the error rate is 1 in $10^9$ base pairs for a human genome of $3 	imes 10^9$ base pairs.
Discuss why 'absolute' consistency in DNA copying would lead to the extinction of a species during environmental shifts.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the mathematical and chemical necessity of variation for the survival of populations (Niche adaptation).
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical explanation of why no biochemical reaction is 100% reliable and its evolutionary significance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Analysis of Regenerative Potential
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between 'True Regeneration' and 'Reproduction' in complex organisms
materials: Case studies on Planaria vs. Starfish vs. Human Liver
steps: Analyze the role of 'Neoblasts' (totipotent stem cells) in Planaria that allow for complete body regeneration.
Compare this to the limited 'healing' or tissue-level regeneration in mammals.
Explain why fragmentation cannot be the primary reproductive strategy for complex organisms with organ-system levels of organization.
Identify the energy cost associated with maintaining a pool of undifferentiated cells.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically analyzes the biological constraints that limit regeneration in highly differentiated multicellular organisms.
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed reasoning on the trade-off between cell specialization and regenerative capacity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Double Fertilization and Triple Fusion Modeling
activities_projects.objective: Map the unique angiosperm process of forming the embryo and endosperm
steps: Trace the path of the two male gametes released by the pollen tube.
Model the 'Syngamy' (Male gamete + Egg $ightarrow$ Zygote) and 'Triple Fusion' (Male gamete + 2 Polar nuclei $ightarrow$ PEN).
Explain the ploidy levels: Zygote ($2n$) and Endosperm ($3n$).
Research why the endosperm develops before the embryo in most seeds.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the complex cytological events that distinguish sexual reproduction in flowering plants.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct representation of the $2n$ and $3n$ structures and their functional roles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hormonal Regulation of the Ovarian Cycle
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the feedback loops between the Pituitary and the Ovary
materials: Hormone concentration charts (FSH, LH, Estrogen, Progesterone)
steps: Plot the levels of the four major hormones over a 28-day cycle.
Identify the 'LH Surge' and correlate it with the timing of ovulation.
Explain the role of the Corpus Luteum in secreting Progesterone to maintain the endometrium.
Describe the 'Negative Feedback' that occurs when fertilization does not take place.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of the endocrine synchronization required for the mammalian reproductive cycle.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the hormone graph and identification of the peak corresponding to ovulation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Energetics of Placental Exchange
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the surface area adaptations for fetal-maternal transport
steps: Research the total surface area of the human placenta (approx. $16 	ext{ m}^2$).
Explain the 'Counter-current' exchange mechanism that optimizes the diffusion of $O_2$ and nutrients.
Calculate the efficiency of waste ($CO_2$ and urea) removal based on concentration gradients.
Discuss the 'Placental Barrier' and list specific substances (like alcohol or certain viruses) that can bypass it.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the physiological sophistication of the placenta as a multi-functional organ (respiratory, nutritive, and excretory).
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of how villi and blood spaces maximize diffusion rates (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Germ Layer Differentiation (Gastrulation)
activities_projects.objective: Trace the origin of organ systems from the three primary germ layers
steps: Identify the formation of Ectoderm, Mesoderm, and Endoderm following implantation.
Map which germ layer gives rise to the Nervous System (Ectoderm), Heart/Muscles (Mesoderm), and Digestive Tract (Endoderm).
Explain the concept of 'Cellular Differentiation' where identical cells start performing different functions.
Research the role of the 'Yolk Sac' in humans compared to birds.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects early embryonic stages to the complex anatomy of the adult organism.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of major organs into their respective germ layer origins (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Male Accessory Glands and Semen Chemistry
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the chemical environment required for sperm motility
materials: Research data on Prostate and Seminal Vesicle secretions
steps: Identify the secretions of the Seminal Vesicles (Fructose) and Prostate Gland (Alkaline fluid).
Explain why Fructose is necessary for sperm (energy source).
Discuss the importance of the alkaline nature of semen in neutralizing the acidic environment of the female reproductive tract.
Explain the role of the Scrotum in thermoregulation ($2	ext{--}2.5^\circ	ext{C}$ lower than body temp).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the integrated function of accessory glands in ensuring the success of internal fertilization.
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical summary of how semen composition aids in sperm survival and transport (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Surgical Contraception: Physiological Impacts
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the bypass of gamete transport without affecting hormonal balance
steps: Diagram the site of incision/ligation in the Vas Deferens and Fallopian Tubes.
Explain why these procedures do not stop the production of Testosterone or Estrogen.
Analyze why 'reversibility' is difficult in surgical methods compared to mechanical barriers.
Discuss the 'Failure Rate' of various contraceptive methods using a comparative table.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between methods that block gamete production, gamete transport, and implantation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct reasoning on why surgical sterilization does not affect secondary sexual characters (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Genetics of Sex Ratio and Social Impact
activities_projects.objective: Mathematically prove the $1:1$ probability of male vs. female offspring
steps: Draw a Punnett square crossing a male ($XY$) and a female ($XX$).
Calculate the percentage chance of a male child ($50\%$).
Argue scientifically why the father determines the sex of the child.
Research the legal implications of the 'Pre-conception and Pre-natal Diagnostic Techniques (PCPNDT) Act'.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates biological sex determination with social ethics and legal frameworks.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate Punnett square and a logical argument against gender-based discrimination (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Pathophysiology of STIs (HIV-AIDS)
activities_projects.objective: Understand the mechanism of viral entry and immune system collapse
steps: Research how HIV targets the helper T-cells (immune system).
Explain the difference between being 'HIV Positive' and having 'AIDS'.
List the four primary modes of transmission (Sexual, Blood, Syringes, Mother-to-child).
Analyze why a condom is the only contraceptive that effectively blocks viral transmission.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the critical importance of reproductive health in preventing global pandemics.
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed explanation of the link between reproductive choices and immunological health (10 marks)
